methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $250M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $539M - $10.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Colossal Biosciences
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $10.32B

private.name: New Limit
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $3.1B

data.narrative:
eGenesis, a Series C-stage biotech company founded by Luhan Yang and George Church, has raised $250M+ to date. The valuation exercise draws on a universe of 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in Biotech & Health, including Colossal Biosciences ($10.3B, Series C), Chai Discovery ($3.8B, Series B), New Limit ($3.1B, Series C), and Eikon Therapeutics ($539M, Series B).

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $250M total raised proxy and applying a 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $425M. Peer-median analysis across the four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $3.5B, with a range spanning $539M to $10.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; the sector median P/S of 6.9x across biotech peers is unavailable for use without target revenue disclosure, typical for private companies.

Synthesizing the two applicable methods produces a triangulated range of $361M (low) to $4.0B (high), with a median of $1.9B. The wide spread reflects material dispersion in peer valuations, particularly the outsized position of Colossal Biosciences. Key uncertainties include stage-of-advancement relative to peers, pipeline maturity, and capital efficiency—variables not fully resolved in publicly available data. Stage-progression may underestimate given peer comparables; peer median may overweight outliers.
